title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In a conversation with Hercule Poirot, a legal professional discusses the disappearance of Olga Seminova, a foreign nurse who cared for Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith, the aunt of Hugo Drake. While Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith had previously intended her estate to pass to Hugo and his wife, a codicil written three weeks before her death diverted a substantial portion of her wealth to Olga. However, handwriting experts determined the codicil was a forgery. Olga had previously been tasked by Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith to imitate her handwriting for private letters, and she attempted to use this skill to pass off the forged document. Following a lawsuit regarding the document's authenticity, Olga disappeared.

Jeremy Fullerton, a lawyer, reflects on his past encounter with Olga two years prior. He recalls her as a passionate, resilient woman from Central Europe. Although he felt compassion for her difficult life, he remained a proponent of the law. Fullerton recalls a meeting where Olga sought his legal representation, claiming Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith left her the money because the woman pitied her suffering and disliked her relatives. Olga argued that Mrs. Drake was overbearing and that her aunt deserved to enjoy her wealth. Olga defended her actions, claiming she was merely being kind by bringing Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith forbidden foods like chocolate and butter, despite medical advice. Fullerton refused to represent her, noting his existing duty to the Drake family.
